water.name: Upper Reese River and tributaries from the Arc Dome Wilderness boundary south of the Yomba Shoshone Tribe Reservation to its headwaters

sources.title: State of Nevada Board of Wildlife Commissioners — Fishing Seasons CR 25-16 (Jan 1, 2026 – Dec 31, 2027)
sources.quotedText: Upper Reese River and tributaries from the Arc Dome Wilderness boundary south of the Yomba Shoshone Tribe Reservation to its headwaters (Nye County): Open year around. Limits — No limit on trout. Hours: Any hour of the day or night.

sources.title: Reese River, Upper — Nevada Department of Wildlife
sources.quotedText: Upper Reese River, Nye County, Southern region. Small wild-trout stream originating in the Arc Dome Wilderness at the southern end of the Toiyabe Range.

sources.title: Lahontan Cutthroat Trout Species Management Plan for the Upper Humboldt River Drainage Basin — NDOW
sources.quotedText: The Reese River subbasin contains multiple Lahontan cutthroat trout recovery populations within the Toiyabe Range headwater streams, including reaches within and adjacent to the Arc Dome Wilderness.

sources.title: Apply & Buy — Nevada Fishing Licenses — NDOW
sources.quotedText: Anyone 12 years of age or older is required to have a fishing license in their possession while in the field.

regulations.humanSummary: This upper Reese River reach and its tributaries are open to fishing year-round.

parameters.counting_rule: No daily or possession limit on trout in this reach; anglers may keep any number of trout caught.

regulations.humanSummary: No limit on trout in this reach of the Reese River and its tributaries.
Fishing is permitted any hour of the day or night in this reach.
regulations.verbatimText: Hours: Any hour of the day or night.

parameters.note: Standard Nevada resident/nonresident fishing license required for anglers 12 and older.

regulations.humanSummary: A Nevada fishing license is required for anglers age 12 and older; no license required under age 12.
regulations.verbatimText: Anyone 12 years of age or older is required to have a fishing license in their possession while in the field.
